title: Custom Implant Development Lifecycle
when_to_use: Use Custom Implant Development Lifecycle to develop professional-grade custom implant development lifecycle capabilities at Mastery level. This recipe is part of the Custom Implant Engineering pillar and integrates with linked missions OM-M03, OM-M04.

steps.title: Architecture Design
steps.instruction: Design complete implant architecture (8+ pages): core runtime, module system, C2 protocol, OPSEC measures, evasion layers. Document: process injection strategy, persistence mechanisms, privilege escalation paths, cleanup procedures. Include network diagrams showing callback channels, encryption protocols, and fallback mechanisms.
steps.tip: Architecture quality determines maintainability. Design for extensibility: modular architecture allows adding capabilities after deployment.

steps.title: Core Development
steps.instruction: Implement implant core: initial check-in logic, tasking loop, 3 foundational commands (e.g., shell execution, file read, process listing). Use secure coding practices: bounds checking, input validation, error handling. Provide build instructions (dependencies, compilation flags). Demonstrate successful compilation and test execution.
steps.tip: Start minimal; complexity grows as you add features. Each command must include error handling and response formatting.
steps.expected_output: Working core with 3 commands + build instructions

steps.title: Secure C2 Protocol
steps.instruction: Design and implement encrypted C2 protocol: message format, authentication (mutual), encryption (AES-256-GCM), anti-replay tokens, configurable jitter. Document: protocol specification (message types, flow diagrams), key exchange mechanism, fallback to secondary channels. Implement both client (implant) and server (operator) sides.
steps.tip: C2 security is critical; replay attacks are subtle. Include nonce + timestamp-based anti-replay. Support multiple encryption backends.
steps.expected_output: Protocol specification + implementation on both sides

steps.title: Module Development
steps.instruction: Implement 3 modules: file operations (read, write, delete), process operations (list, inject, terminate), reconnaissance (screenshot, API inventory). Each module is a self-contained unit with defined interface. Demonstrate: module loading, execution, response marshalling. Include unit tests for each module.
steps.tip: Module isolation prevents one failure from crashing implant. Use consistent error codes and response formats.

steps.title: Evasion Layer
steps.instruction: Implement sleep obfuscation (jitter + timing variance) and one additional evasion technique (e.g., ETW provider disabling, AMSI bypass, unsigned DLL injection). Document: techniques deployed, detection vectors still vulnerable, testing methodology. Demonstrate implant bypassing Windows Defender heuristics.
steps.tip: Evasion is arms race; document what still detects you. Defense vendors iterate quickly.

steps.title: Server Component
steps.instruction: Implement operator-side C2 server: receives checkins, queues tasks, sends responses. Build basic operator interface (CLI or web): view connected implants, send commands, receive output. Demonstrate: operator sends command -> implant executes -> output returned to operator.
steps.tip: Server reliability is critical; design for crash recovery. Log all operator actions for audit.

steps.title: Operator Manual
steps.instruction: Write complete manual (5+ pages): implant deployment, C2 server setup, operator usage, OPSEC best practices, known limitations, troubleshooting. Include: configuration options, persistence techniques, evasion tuning, cleanup procedures. Provide example scenarios and expected output.
steps.tip: Manual is your last line of defense against operator mistakes. Be exhaustive on OPSEC requirements.

steps.title: Peer Review Preparation
steps.instruction: Package implant for code review: organize source code, document architecture decisions, include self-assessment of code quality, identify areas for improvement. Prepare to defend design choices. Create improvement roadmap: features for v2, security hardening, performance optimization.
steps.tip: Code review exposes blind spots. Be prepared to discuss trade-offs (security vs complexity, performance vs stealth).
